title: carex arenicola subsp. pansa
intro: Carex arenicola subsp. pansa, also known as Pansa Sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, typically found in coastal areas. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Carex arenicola subsp. pansa include potential anti-cancer and antimicrobial activities, as well as the ability to aid in wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
